subject: Human Resources
title: HR Ethics and Confidentiality
description: One-minute skill: HR Ethics and Confidentiality.

hook.text: An HR manager learns an employee has a terminal illness. The employee's boss asks why they've been absent.

buildup.text: HR handles the most sensitive information in any company—salaries, medical records, investigations, and terminations.

discovery.text: Confidentiality isn't optional. Leaking private information can destroy trust and trigger lawsuits.

twist.text: Sometimes confidentiality conflicts with transparency—HR must navigate these tensions carefully every day.

climax.text: Ethical HR builds a foundation of trust. Without it, employees won't report problems or seek help.

punchline.text: Trust is HR's currency. Confidentiality protects it.

quiz.question: Why is confidentiality critical in HR?
options: It builds trust and protects sensitive employee information
It makes HR seem more powerful
It's only important for legal compliance
